payload.title: Rolling revenue-equivalent totals under-count during the first minutes after deploy (warm-up window).
payload.expected_behavior: For any requested window size `W`, if fewer than `W` samples exist, the API should return the **sum of all samples currently in the buffer** (the entire partial window).
payload.actual_behavior: While the buffer is still filling, the API returns a total that **omits the most recent sample** (the freshest minute). Once `len(samples) >= W`, totals match manual sums.

environment.- **Deploy: Canary 12% → observed on `canary-az-b` only (same code path as stable)

hints: Hit `GET /metrics/window?minutes=15` during the first ~3–8 minutes after a cold start or cache flush.
Compare against `GET /metrics/minutes?limit=15` (non-aggregating list) — totals diverge until the buffer fills.
Library symbol likely named `RollingMetrics` or similar; look for **partial buffer** / **warm-up** handling in window sum logic.
hypotheses: partial buffer / warm-up branch mishandles tail slice

bug_summary.symptoms: After deploying **bug_fix** `v2.3.0-canary`, the **15-minute rolling tile** on the executive dashboard reads **~20–37% below** the sum of per-minute ingest values shown in the raw drill-down table. The discrepancy **disappears** once enough minutes have accumulated (after the window is full). Finance noticed first because the tile feeds a reconciliation export.
bug_summary.scope: bug_fix dashboard rolling aggregate API + bug_fix library

payload.root_cause: In last_n_sum, the branch for len(self._buf) < n uses sum(self._buf[:-1]), which drops the newest sample during buffer warm-up. The contract requires summing all buffered samples when fewer than n exist.
payload.approach: Replace partial-buffer return with sum(self._buf). Add regression test for warm-up window (already embodied by the generated repro). Optionally add property test: for all n >= len(buf), last_n_sum(n) == sum(buf).
risks: If any caller relied on the buggy omission (unlikely), changing semantics could alter dashboards briefly.
Large buffers: sum() is O(k); acceptable for stated capacities.
validation_plan: Run pytest on mini_repo/tests and the generated repro (expect pass after fix).
Manual: simulate cold start, query window endpoint before buffer fills, compare to raw list sum.
Add edge case tests: n==0, n==1, single push with large n.

edge_cases: Very large n relative to capacity — FIFO eviction interacts with window semantics
Floating-point accumulation on long streams (precision vs finance use case)
requests_more_evidence: Confirm which traceback corresponds to prod vs shadow pods in logs.

repro_steps: Expected before fix: failing assertion on warm-window total (47.5 vs actual).
payload.root_cause_hypothesis: In last_n_sum, the branch for len(self._buf) < n uses sum(self._buf[:-1]), which drops the newest sample during buffer warm-up. The contract requires summing all buffered samples when fewer than n exist.

patch_plan.approach: Replace partial-buffer return with sum(self._buf). Add regression test for warm-up window (already embodied by the generated repro). Optionally add property test: for all n >= len(buf), last_n_sum(n) == sum(buf).
risks: If any caller relied on the buggy omission (unlikely), changing semantics could alter dashboards briefly.
Large buffers: sum() is O(k); acceptable for stated capacities.
patch_plan.optional_unified_diff_hint: In last_n_sum partial branch, replace sum(self._buf[:-1]) with sum(self._buf).

tests_to_add: Regression: repro test merged into mini_repo/tests.
regression_checks: Run pytest on mini_repo/tests and the generated repro (expect pass after fix).
Manual: simulate cold start, query window endpoint before buffer fills, compare to raw list sum.
Add edge case tests: n==0, n==1, single push with large n.

Confirm canary vs stable log streams share identical bug_fix wheel hash.
Verify finance export uses same code path as dashboard tile.
